description: Requests a representation of the specified resource. Should only retrieve data and should not have other effects.
Identical to GET but without the response body. Useful for checking resource existence or retrieving headers before downloading.
Submits data to the specified resource, often causing a state change or side effects on the server. Used for creating new resources.
Replaces all current representations of the target resource with the request payload. Creates the resource if it does not exist.
Applies partial modifications to a resource. Unlike PUT, PATCH is not idempotent by default, though a PATCH request can be made idempotent.
Describes the communication options for the target resource. Used in CORS preflight requests to determine allowed methods and headers.
Establishes a tunnel to the server identified by the target resource. Used primarily for HTTPS through an HTTP proxy.
Performs a message loop-back test along the path to the target resource. Useful for debugging, but often disabled for security reasons.
